title: Stemodiacra tomentosa
intro: Stemodiacra tomentosa, also known as Mina, is a plant species native to Mexico, belonging to the Asteraceae family and is characterized by its hairy leaves and stems. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been shown to provide relief from pain, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Stemodiacra tomentosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Stemodiacra tomentosa is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems.

The classification of Stemodiacra tomentosa is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Rosids, order Celastrales, family Celastraceae, genus Stemodiacra, species S. tomentosa. The plant is valued for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of various health issues.
